sources.name: MoSPI National Accounts Statistics — consolidated annual + quarterly NAS series (data.gov.in mirror)
sources.authority: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (National Statistical Office)

license.name: Government of India Open Data License

indicator.title: National Gross Value Added by industry, constant 2011-12 prices (quarterly)
indicator.description: Quarterly Gross Value Added by 17-industry tier at constant 2011-12 prices. Time field is YYYY-MM at the quarter's start month (Q1 = YYYY-04, Q2 = YYYY-07, Q3 = YYYY-10, Q4 = (Y+1)-01). The high-frequency companion to the annual series; useful for monsoon / supply-shock narratives.
indicator.methodology_vintage: MoSPI National Accounts Statistics, base 2011-12, constant prices. Consolidated CSV ingested 2026-05-14; per (indicator, industry, quarter) the most-final published revision tier was selected (rank order: First Advance < Second Advance < Provisional < First Revised < Second Revised < Third Revised < Final < Additional Revision) and stamped on each row via the v1.3 `vintage` field. Source CSV included a parallel current-price series and a 2022-23 rebased series; both were intentionally dropped — constant-price 2011-12 is yen-gov's canonical real-GDP/GVA basis (matches IMF WEO, World Bank WDI NY.GDP.MKTP.KD, OECD SNA, MoSPI press notes). Pre-computed growth-rate rows were also dropped: MoSPI uses fixed-base accounts so naive (Y_t - Y_{t-1}) / Y_{t-1} on this constant-price level series reproduces the published growth rate to the last decimal. Subindustry rows (Crops, Livestock, Air Transport, etc.) were also dropped — citizen front door uses the 17-industry NIC-1 tier; subindustry returns in a Phase F deep-dive.
indicator.notes: Values are in ₹ crore at 2011-12 prices — comparable across years (real growth is meaningful) but NOT directly comparable to today's market value. For 'size of the economy in today's money' use the current-price series (not yet shipped at the national level).
